text: As believers, we are expected to be in a posture of gratitude towards God because of His sovereign gracious working in our lives. The aim of this study was to examine the theological appreciation of thanksgiving based on 1 Corinthians 1:4-9. Giving thanks is an act that is seen by the Apostle Paul as a way of building and evaluating the faith of the early Christians in Corinth. It appears that the Apostle Paul gives thanks to God because He is the One who is the sovereign Saviour that calls believers from the state of spiritual death into the life of grace. This qualitative descriptive research attempted to explore how Paul’s instruction given in 1 Corinthians 1:4-9 was utilised for the strengthening of the early Christian church in Corinth, and most importantly, how it can continue to function as a paradigm for Christians to live in today’s day and age. The results of this research demonstrate that Paul exhorts the Corinthians to live confidently as called saints of God, and to take an attitude of thankfulness towards the vast spiritual gifts they had been endowed with through Christ Jesus. Overall, it was found that the model of thanksgiving that the Apostle Paul gives for God’s preserving grace, which is the theme of the entire chapter, is just as applicable to the lives of contemporary believers in terms of their spiritual survival and victory.
